Enchanted Elven Chain Tunic  
 


Slot: CHEST
AC: 14
WIS: +4 INT: +4
SV MAGIC: +2
WT: 1.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except SHD MNK SHM NEC BST BER
Race: ELF HIE HEF FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Slot: CHEST
AC: 14
WIS: +4 INT: +4
SV MAGIC: +2
WT: 1.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except SHD MNK SHM NEC BST BER
Race: ELF HIE HEF FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Chain
Tint:

Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Average Price: 650pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 1
[Comments ]

Crafted: This item is crafted by players.

    • Class: ALL
    Expansion: Not Set
    Yield: 1 x Enchanted Elven Chain Tunic


Uploaded November 27th, 2008

▲ Page top
Ingredients
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2001 at 3:33 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361079961
View User Forum
101 posts
To make this you need the following:
Enchanted Elven Chain Tunic = Elven Smithy Hammer + Chainmail Gloves Tunic + Silk Swatch + Moonlight Temper + 3 Enchanted Mithril Ring

Good Luck,
Erollisi Marr
▲ Page top
ouch
QuoteReply
# May 22 2001 at 8:57 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Well I've attempted this several times on the Innoruuk server and have yet to be successful. I gotta say its MUCH easier now that blue diamonds are NOT required for enchanting the mithril. However, the biggest headache is finding the swirling mists. These things are pretty rare drops in Steamfont and Najena. So far I've probably killed 100 mist and steam elementals and have looted about 10 swirling mists. I see the elementals spawn about once every half hour or so in either zone. The essence of moonlight aren't too bad...they seem to drop about 60% of the time off the little hunchback vampire mobs in Mistmoore. (will pillager, thought corruptor, etc.)

Not too bad...but the craft requires some serious skill. Considering the time and effort put into gathering the components...I wouldn't attempt the craft unless your smithing skill is 200+. I've seen the enchanted chain bracers and veils...those can be crafted (with luck) if your skill is about 150.

Laelia Jadebreaker
55 enchantress of Erollisi Marr
Innoruuk Server
▲ Page top
RE: ouch
QuoteReply
# Jul 03 2001 at 1:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763353268
2 posts
I finally made one of these with skill 175. It took me 4 attempts. I'm pretty sure the trivial chart on these items is on the same relative scale as banded. I think the bracer or gorget is easiest...up through the cloak being the hardest.

The texture is light blue with some dark grainy black. It looks nice...but the look is probably much better if you have a full suit of it. The tunic with drake hide leggings and insidious sleeves looks a bit strange. I look almost like a newbie...which is somewhat depressing considering the amount of effort it takes to make this armor.

I'm probably going to keep crafting it until I can make the enchanted mithril plate. If you gather the components for a moonlight temper I will attempt any piece for the cost of the metals.
Just give me a tell in game if you're on Inny server.
▲ Page top

# Mar 02 2001 at 12:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756915055
5 posts
ill be attempting to craft this as soon as i can get the tempers made. if you have any of the three ingredients for the tempers i will always be buying them......essence of moonlight from mistmoore,morning dew foraged in lesser faydark, swirling mist dropped off of steam or mist elementals in steamfont or najena. it will take about 12 of each to craft this item . my mages name is Tomamaru. give her a tell if you have any of the tempers needed to make this. if her skill is great tenough she may be able to craft it for you.

▲ Page top
Cost = Lots of Cash and Time and Effort.
QuoteReply
# Jan 28 2001 at 11:35 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591754816097
4 posts
Ok, first off don't expect to find anyone selling one of these. If you do, expect to pay lots of money. 6,000pp is not unreasonable for this, in fact, it may even be too cheap. Here's the recipe:

Combine the following items in Koada'dal Forge in Felwithe.
note: you must be a high elf to make this armor.

tunic chainmail pattern
elven smithy hammer
silk swatch
moonlight temper
3 enchanted mithril rings

tailoring skill (combine in sewing kit)
SILK SWATCH = Spider Silk + Spider Silk

brewing skill (combine in brew barrel)
MOONLIGHT TEMPER = 2 Essence of Moonlight + 1 Morning Dew + 1 Swirling Mist
Essence of Moonlight is an uncommon drop off vampires in Mistmoore and undead in Kitichor Woods
Morning Dew is foraged with forage skill of 100+ or found at 6:00am all around the Faydark
Swirling Mist is found off air elmentals in Steamfont, Najena and the Hole.

ENCHANTED MITHRIL RINGS = 1 Enchanted Large Brick of Mithril + Morning Dew + File
Enchanted Large Brick of Mithril is created by having an enchanter cast Enchant Mithril on a brick of mithril. Enchant Mithril requires a Blue Diamond as a component
Blue Diamonds are found off lvl 50+ mobs. They sell for ~750pp now because enchanter can now use them for jewel craft. This is the hardest part of this armor. Getting those blue diamonds. You need 3 for each Tunic.

So hit that combine button and if you're lucky you'll get a nice Enchanted Elven Chainmail Tunic. It took me 3 tries over 2 months to finally succeed with a skill of 186. The cost for each try was around 2000pp each. Now with the higher blue diamon prices the cost will probably be 3500pp per try. I don't think anyone would be wiling to sell one seeing all the effort required to collect all the ingredients to make one of these bad boys. BTW, only High Elves, Half Elves and Wood Elves can wear this armor, but any class can wear it. Awesome for druids and pure casters!


▲ Page top

▲ Page top
How to make this.
QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2001 at 1:37 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591752703564
Scholar
36 posts
High elf smith with a skill well over 175, three blue diamonds used to enchant three mithril bars per attempt (NOT trivial at skill 200, which is a beast to get to anyway), along with assorted smithing apparatus (hammer, backings etc) that the smith can gather. Plus, several batches of temper, which require drops from multiple areas including essence of moonlight, a semi rare drop from mistmoore. Can it be done? certainly. But be prepared to pay many k plat or come up with 6 to 12 blue diamonds plus a chunk of cash for the effort required. See www.eqtraders.com for full details on the racial smithing.
▲ Page top
RE: Excellent Post
QuoteReply
# Jan 10 2001 at 10:35 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592246278742
Scholar
31 posts
Thanks for the great information. Anyone (High Elf) on Veeshan working this yet. Post so we can support your effort and compensate you for your time and talent.

Mangudi
